title: Algiers-1957: An Approach to Urban Counterinsurgency
author_or_org: Maj. Robert J. Kee / Army University Press
pages_or_sections: online text; setting the scene, strike, population control
strengths: Accessible source with concrete force-presence figures and detailed description of the urban theater.
limitations: Retrospective military analysis; not a victim registry and not neutral on methods.
notes: Primary backbone for the Battle of Algiers theater description and force headcount inputs.

title: Urban insurgency in the twenty-first century
strengths: Recent scholarly synthesis with concise, citable numeric statements on force deployment and operation duration.
limitations: Short synthetic passage rather than a dedicated casualty reconstruction.
notes: Used for the 4,600 paratrooper deployment and Oct. 8 endpoint.

title: Population des quinze départements algériens
author_or_org: Service de l'Information, Délégation Générale du Gouvernement
strengths: Official demographic tabulation on a 1954 census base.
limitations: Provides arrondissement and department totals, not the narrower commune/agglomeration values needed for lower bounds.
notes: Used as the high civilian denominator bound and to avoid whole-country exposure.

title: Alger
author_or_org: French Wikipedia (search snippet used as a tertiary restatement of historical census data)
pages_or_sections: population history snippet in search result
strengths: Accessible restatement of commune and banlieue counts absent from the official arrondissement table recovered here.
limitations: Tertiary source; retained only as a denominator bracketing aid and cross-checked against the official arrondissement figure.
notes: Used conservatively for low/best civilian bounds, with quality tier downgraded accordingly.

title: La bataille d’Alger
strengths: Recent scholarly synthesis with explicit statement that Massu had 10,000 soldiers in sector Alger-Sahel in addition to police and gendarmerie-type forces.
limitations: Still synthetic and does not by itself resolve victim-status classification.
notes: Used as the high military exposure ceiling.

title: Cairn search snippet citing Massu, La vraie bataille d’Alger
strengths: Provides the best easily recoverable citation path for the oft-repeated '300 killed' claim and flags its source pages in Massu.
limitations: Snippet-level access only; the underlying figure concerns losses in the ZAA/terrorist organization and is not explicit enough to isolate armed combatants.
notes: Kept as context only; not promoted directly into mil_deaths_direct.

title: Le terrorisme urbain dans la guerre d'Algérie
strengths: Accessible citation path for the common 751 attacks / 314 dead / 917 wounded tally.
limitations: Broader than the Jan-Oct 1957 police-power episode; status of the 314 dead is not cleanly split into civilians versus police/other organized personnel.
notes: Retained as mixed-status context, not as a core episode numerator.

title: La disparition forcée durant la Guerre d’Indépendance algérienne: Le projet Mille autres, ou les disparus de la « bataille d’Alger » (1957)
strengths: High-quality scholarly discussion of the forced-disappearance archive base.
limitations: Not a direct-death count; documents family searches for disappeared rather than verified fatalities.
notes: Used to justify why disappearances are analytically central yet should remain outside the baseline direct-death series.

title: HISTOIRE D’UN FICHIER SECRET – Alger 1957 – des Maurice Audin par milliers
pages_or_sections: main text around the 3,024 and 1,010 figures; note [65]
geographic_scope: Grand Alger / Battle of Algiers disappearances
strengths: Named-case project with archival grounding; provides the best accessible path to the 1,010-name publication and Teitgen-related disappearance context.
limitations: Still a disappearance project, not a direct-death registry, and the 3,024 figure is explicitly described as hypothetical.
notes: Supports the decision to keep disappearances outside core direct-death metrics.

title: The Road to Algiers
pages_or_sections: pp. 36-37 (attack/reprisal totals in the Philippeville area)
strengths: Convenient scholarly summary of the North-Constantinois escalation with separated victim categories for the initial attacks.
limitations: Not a denominator study; reprisal numbers remain highly disputed.
notes: Used to justify treating August 1955 as a separate episode.

title: Border Control and Insurgent Tactics
pages_or_sections: introductory description of the Morice Line
strengths: Clear scholarly description of the Morice Line as a major border-fortification shift beginning in September 1957.
limitations: Not an Algiers city source and not a casualty dataset.
notes: Used only to justify the post-Algiers border/rural episode split.

title: The Algerian War: A Model for Counterinsurgency Operations
strengths: Accessible scholarly discussion of the 1959 shift to large-scale mop-up operations under Challe.
limitations: High-level operational narrative rather than subnational exposure reconstruction.
notes: Used to justify the rural/consolidation episode after urban Algiers.
